SEARCH
Package JoyLuck.newHistorism

Class HistoricalEnvironment

java.lang.Object
JoyLuck.newHistorism.HistoricalEnvironment
public class HistoricalEnvironment extends Object
Defines historical and political contexts that determine the behaviors of characters in The Joy Luck Club.

Every method in ChineseMother is compiled inside one of the Chinese environments below. Every LegacySurvivalModeException occurs when those methods are executed inside CAPITALISM_AMERICA.

See Also:
  • ChineseMother
  • AmericanDaughter
  • LegacySurvivalModeException

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    AMERICAN_DREAM
    Life should be better, richer and fuller for everyone regardless of their social status, which attracted a lot of immigrants in the 1950s in search for a better life for their future generations.
    static final String
    CAPITALISM_AMERICA
    The modern American society in the 1980s that valued individualism, freedom, and capitalism.
    static final String
    CONCUBINE_CHINA
    Concubinage system: In China between 1920 and 1930, males could have multiple concubines, who were considered inferior to the primary wife, with their status declining based on their numbers.
    static final String
    PATRIARCHAL_CHINA
    Feudal patriarchy: In 1920s China, women had little agency, often forced into submission, concubinage, or arranged marriage at a very young age.
    static final String
    WARTIME_CHINA
    Second Sino- Japanese War: A violent eight-year period (1937-1945) where China was invaded by Japan.

  • Field Details

    • WARTIME_CHINA

      public static final String WARTIME_CHINA
      Second Sino- Japanese War: A violent eight-year period (1937-1945) where China was invaded by Japan. Over 20 million Chinese citizens were killed or displaced by the invasion and it left a deep generational trauma.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • PATRIARCHAL_CHINA

      public static final String PATRIARCHAL_CHINA
      Feudal patriarchy: In 1920s China, women had little agency, often forced into submission, concubinage, or arranged marriage at a very young age.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values
